memories.memory_text: I popped through the roof of the SUV, heart racing, and rushed to position the magnetometer as the homemade panels unfolded around me—I could feel the desert night air on my face and the urgency in every second ticking by.
grounded_facts: Jane emerges through the roof of the SUV to activate her equipment. She positions a magnetometer cobbled from spare parts. The astronomical devices are mounted on foldable roof panels. She feels urgency
noting the anomaly is late. The scene takes place at night in the desert. She interacts directly with the equipment and environment.

memories.memory_text: I pulled on my father's old gloves—too big for my hands—and when Selvig said he'd be proud of me, I felt a lump in my throat; I didn't expect that, not out here under the stars, not while waiting for something impossible to appear.
grounded_facts: Jane retrieves and wears a pair of old

oversized gloves. Darcy hands her the gloves and champagne from inside the SUV. Selvig recognizes the gloves and comments on her father's pride. Jane reacts with a sad smile after his comment. The gloves are masculine and do not fit her well. The moment occurs just before scanning the sky for the anomaly.

memories.memory_text: I stood with half my body out of the SUV’s roof, filming the swirling light storm ahead—those patterns weren’t natural, I could feel it in my bones.
grounded_facts: Jane is partially outside the SUV through the open roof panel. She is recording video of the cosmic light storm. She is visually focused on the phenomenon ahead. Her posture and position indicate active documentation. The light storm is visible and striking enough to inspire awe. The roof panels of the SUV are open. The SUV is in motion toward the event. The phenomenon is described as a 'light storm'.

memories.memory_text: The SUV hit a bump and I nearly flew out—suddenly I was falling, then yanked back by Selvig, my heart slamming against my ribs.
grounded_facts: The SUV hits a bump while in motion. Jane loses balance and nearly falls from the vehicle. Selvig physically grabs and pulls her back inside. Jane experiences a sudden moment of physical danger. The event causes a strong physiological reaction (fear

adrenaline). The open roof allows for the possibility of falling out. Selvig is present and reacts quickly to prevent injury. Jane is not secured inside the vehicle. The terrain is uneven. The SUV is moving at speed. Jane is aware of Selvig’s intervention.

memories.memory_text: I felt the rush of excitement seeing the phenomenon light up the sky—I couldn't help but grin and shout, 'Isn't this great?!' It was real, and I could finally see it with my own eyes.
grounded_facts: Jane expresses exhilaration upon witnessing the atmospheric phenomenon.
She speaks aloud in a moment of emotional intensity, indicating personal investment.

memories.memory_text: Right after, a doubt hit me—I turned to Selvig and asked if he saw it too, needing to know I wasn't losing my mind. His joke didn't reassure me, but at least he didn't deny it.
grounded_facts: Jane seeks confirmation from Selvig about the phenomenon's visibility.
She explicitly questions her own sanity, showing uncertainty despite prior confidence.
Selvig responds with a quip but does not refute her observation.

memories.memory_text: I was leaning out of the moving SUV, camera in hand, filming the tornado when a deafening crack split the air—lightning exploded through the center of the funnel, so bright and violent it felt like the sky had split open.
grounded_facts: Jane was in a moving SUV driving toward a tornado.
She was leaning out the window while recording the event.
A loud and intense lightning bolt struck through the center of the funnel cloud.
The lightning strike occurred suddenly, immediately after the SUV disturbed two ravens.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed the steering wheel and yanked it hard when Darcy tried to turn away—I couldn't let her leave, not yet, not without knowing what was out there.
grounded_facts: Jane physically intervened by grabbing the wheel to prevent Darcy from driving away.
There was a struggle between Jane and Darcy for control of the SUV.

memories.memory_text: I turned the wheel hard to avoid the man in the road, but the SUV swerved too late.
grounded_facts: Jane turned the wheel hard to avoid a man in the road.
The SUV swerved but it was too late to avoid hitting him.

memories.memory_text: I felt the SUV jolt violently as something hit the side—there was a sickening thud, and then the car skidded to a stop. I don’t know what I hit, but it felt like a person.
grounded_facts: Jane Foster was driving the SUV when it collided with a man.
The impact caused a loud thud and sent the man flying.
The SUV skidded to a stop immediately after the collision.

memories.memory_text: I cupped his face in my hands and begged him to open his eyes—I remember the relief when he groaned and those blue eyes finally focused on mine. We just stared, and for a second, I forgot how to breathe.
grounded_facts: Jane kneels beside the man and cups his face while urging him to open his eyes.
The man groans, his eyes flutter open, and they lock eyes, creating a breathless moment.
Jane experiences a strong emotional reaction upon making eye contact.

memories.memory_text: After Darcy came back with the kit and broke the moment, I looked up at the storm vanishing and suddenly wondered—where did he even come from? The storm was gone, and so was any clear answer.
grounded_facts: The storm evaporates above them after the man regains consciousness.
Jane observes the disappearing storm and expresses confusion about the man's origin.
She voices the question 'Where did he come from?' as a distinct cognitive shift.

memories.memory_text: I knelt beside him, telling him not to be dead, urging him to open his eyes — and when he finally did, he was just... this huge, disoriented man staring at me like he didn’t know where he was.
grounded_facts: Jane approached Thor after the SUV hit him.
She spoke to him before he regained consciousness, saying: 'Come on, big guy. Do me a favor and don't be dead, okay? Open your eyes and look at me.'
She saw Thor open his eyes and looked directly at him in concern.

memories.memory_text: I saw the pattern in the sand — faint, but deliberate — and grabbed my camera fast, knowing the wind was already starting to erase it.
grounded_facts: Jane noticed a pattern etched into the sand around the landing site.
She used a flashlight to examine it and called Selvig over to look.
She took photos of the runes before they were erased by the breeze.

memories.memory_text: I gave them my name and number, even though I said I didn't know him—I just couldn't walk away and leave him there alone.
grounded_facts: Jane provides her name, 'Jane Foster,' and contact information to the admissions nurse for Thor's hospital records.
Jane explicitly states she has never met Thor before, despite being the one to report his name and take responsibility for his condition.
Jane mentions tasering Thor and that she only grazed him with the car, indicating her direct involvement in the incident.

memories.memory_text: I showed Erik the star chart through the bubble in the clouds and said, 'These aren't our stars'—I could barely hold back my excitement because this was proof, real evidence of something beyond Earth's sky.
grounded_facts: Jane presents a print-out of constellations seen through the Bifrost cloud to Selvig. She compares them to a star chart labeled for their quadrant and time of year. She states that the stars visible are not the expected ones
implying extraterrestrial or extradimensional origin. She expresses visible excitement while making the observation.

memories.memory_text: When I asked Erik to call in a favor with his contact at LIGO, I felt a surge of hope—this was the first time he treated my work as real science, not just fringe theory.
grounded_facts: Jane asks Selvig if he can contact someone at LIGO to check for gravitational wave data. She proposes that their observatory might have recorded evidence during the previous night's event. Selvig had just agreed to collaborate
shifting from observer to participant. Jane had been waiting for this kind of validation from him.

memories.memory_text: I told the nurse I was his wife, my voice steady even though my heart was racing, and for a second I saw doubt in her eyes—Darcy tried not to laugh, but I couldn't afford to hesitate.
grounded_facts: Jane claims to be Thor's wife to gain visitation access.
The admissions nurse expresses skepticism, recalling Jane previously said she didn't know Thor.
Darcy is present and reacts with a stifled snicker.
Jane improvises an explanation about not truly knowing her husband, referencing marital distance.

memories.memory_text: I stepped inside and saw Thor's bed was empty.
grounded_facts: Jane enters the hospital room and observes that Thor is not in his bed.

memories.memory_text: I said we had to find him—no matter how long it took—because he was the only one who could explain what that thing was, and I wasn’t letting go of the evidence just because it vanished.
grounded_facts: Jane declares 'We find him' in response to losing her evidence.
She insists the search must continue 'however long it takes'.
She believes Thor may have answers about the mysterious phenomenon.

memories.memory_text: I put the SUV in reverse and backed up, then suddenly—BAM—I hit something, or someone… and there he was again, in hospital scrubs, just like before.
grounded_facts: Jane reverses the SUV and collides with Thor.
The impact occurs immediately after she declares the search.
Thor is seen wearing stolen hospital scrubs after the collision.
She directly experiences the physical impact and visual recognition.

memories.memory_text: I saw Thor holding that t-shirt with Donald's name on it, and I had to rip the sticker off fast—my ex's name staring back at me like that, in front of everyone.
grounded_facts: Thor picked up a t-shirt with a sticker that said 'HELLO, MY NAME IS DR. DONALD BLAKE'.
Jane ripped off the sticker and identified the name as her ex.
She felt the need to explain the shirt was the only one that would fit Thor.

memories.memory_text: I pointed to the photo of him in the Bifrost and asked what he was doing there—then wrote down 'Bifrost' in my notebook when he said it, but he wouldn't answer me directly, just leaned in too close, looking at my notes.
grounded_facts: Jane pointed to Thor's form in the Bifrost image on the wall and asked him about it.
She wrote the word 'Bifrost' in her notebook after Selvig repeated it and Thor used it.
Thor moved close to her and looked at her notebook, making her uncomfortable before she closed it.

memories.memory_text: I said sorry to Izzy for the broken mug and offered to pay for it in Spanish, then told Thor he should ask nicely instead of throwing things.
grounded_facts: Jane says 'Sorry, Izzy. Little accident.' and offers to pay for the broken mug in Spanish.
Jane tells Thor he should ask nicely for another coffee instead of throwing the mug.

memories.memory_text: I noticed the same people from the crater walk in, tired and disheveled, and I realized they might know something about the crash site—this could be my chance to learn more.
grounded_facts: Townies enter the diner, described as bedraggled and recognized from the crater scene.
Jane observes them entering and sitting at the counter, indicating a moment of recognition and potential investigative opportunity.

memories.memory_text: I almost believed him—I almost said yes—when he promised me all the answers if I took him to the crater, but Selvig pulled me aside, scared, insisting Thor was dangerous, and I couldn't risk it, not with everything on the line.
grounded_facts: Thor offered to give Jane all the answers she sought in exchange for taking him to the crater.
Jane was visibly conflicted and nearly swayed by Thor's sincerity.
Selvig intervened, expressing fear and warning Jane that Thor was delusional and dangerous.
Jane ultimately refused Thor's request, saying, 'I'm sorry. I can't take you.'

memories.memory_text: I shouted at Coulson, telling him this was my life, that we were on the verge of understanding something extraordinary—he had no right to take it all.
grounded_facts: Jane confronted Agent Coulson
expressing outrage over the seizure of her equipment and data.
She declared that the research was her life and emphasized its scientific importance.
She held up her notebook and stated that everything she knew about the phenomenon was in it and the lab.

memories.memory_text: I was holding my notebook, still speaking, when one of his agents stepped forward and just took it—ripped it from my hands like I didn’t even matter.
grounded_facts: After Jane held up her notebook to emphasize its importance
an agent acting on Coulson's gesture seized it from her.
She was stunned by the physical removal of the notebook
which had contained critical research data.
The action occurred immediately after her declaration and without further dialogue.

memories.memory_text: I leaned on the horn and called out to him, 'You still want a lift?'—not sure if he’d remember me, but hoping he’d take the chance.
grounded_facts: Jane is in her vehicle, the Pinzgauer, parked across the street from the pet store.
She sees Thor inside the pet store and calls out to him.
She offers him a ride, referencing a prior interaction implied by the phrasing 'still want'.

memories.memory_text: I was driving, hands tight on the wheel, heart racing—not just from the speed, but from the fact that I was actually doing this, chasing danger instead of data, and Thor kept looking at me like I was something worth seeing.
grounded_facts: Jane is driving the Pinzgauer with Thor beside her.
She feels nervous and excited, admitting she's never done anything like this before.
She becomes momentarily distracted by Thor's gaze, causing the vehicle to lurch.
She regains control and apologizes, indicating awareness of her lapse.

memories.memory_text: I asked him who he really was, voice shaking a little, because he kept talking about 'realms' and 'rainbow bridges,' and part of me wanted to believe him even as I whispered, 'God, I hope you're not crazy.'
grounded_facts: Jane questions Thor's identity and strange way of speaking.
She expresses uncertainty about whether he is 'good strange or bad strange.'
Thor mentions 'this Realm' and a 'rainbow bridge,' which she tries to interpret scientifically.
She voices doubt about his sanity while still engaging with his claims.

memories.memory_text: I crouched low on the ridge as the spotlight swept past, heart pounding, and whispered into my phone that if Selvig didn't hear from me again, he should come to the crater—because I'd gone and done exactly what he told me not to.
grounded_facts: Jane ducked down to avoid a spotlight sweeping the ridge.
She called Dr. Selvig and left a voicemail while hiding.
In the message, she instructed Selvig to come to the crater site if she disappeared.
She admitted to doing 'what you said I shouldn't do.'

memories.memory_text: I started moving down the ridge to reach him, but then the spotlight swept past and I heard dogs and flashlights coming—I had to turn back fast.
grounded_facts: Jane decides to go help Thor by crossing over the ridge.
A spotlight passes near her, and she hears SHIELD agents and dogs approaching with flashlights.
She retreats quickly upon realizing she's been discovered.

memories.memory_text: I saw the illustration of the hammer in Darcy's hands and recognized it immediately—the same symbol from the crater, the same energy pattern. This wasn't myth; it was data pointing to something real.
grounded_facts: Jane sees an illustration of Thor's hammer in a book held by Darcy.
Jane connects the hammer image to her prior observations of the cosmic event and crater site.
She questions Selvig about the book's origin, indicating recognition and significance.

memories.memory_text: When Darcy backed me up about the Vikings possibly worshipping advanced beings, I felt it—finally someone else saw the pattern. Even Selvig couldn't argue with the logic, not completely.
grounded_facts: Darcy suggests that primitive cultures like the Vikings might have worshipped advanced beings as deities.
Jane expresses gratitude and affirmation toward Darcy's input, indicating emotional and intellectual validation.
Selvig does not refute the idea, though skeptical, and acknowledges Jane's determination.

memories.memory_text: I took my notebook back from Thor, and for a moment, I felt like I could keep going—like I hadn’t lost everything after all.
grounded_facts: Thor returned Jane's notebook to her on the roof of Smith Motors.
Jane expressed surprise and gratitude upon receiving it, saying 'I don't believe it...' and 'Thank you. This means I don't have to start from scratch.'
She opened the notebook eagerly, showing emotional investment in reclaiming her research.

memories.memory_text: Thor drew lines in my notebook, connecting points like branches, and said science and magic are the same—our worlds are linked by something called Yggdrasil. I didn’t know what to believe, but I saw the Hubble images in a new way.
grounded_facts: Thor added to Jane's sketch of the Bifrost in her notebook, drawing connections between points in space.
He explained that science and magic are one and the same, and that her world is part of the Nine Realms connected by Yggdrasil.
Jane reacted with amazement and intrigue as she watched him draw and listened to his explanation.
Thor referenced her Hubble Telescope data as indirect observations of Yggdrasil.

memories.memory_text: I helped Thor and Selvig get the townspeople into cars and inside buildings, trying to keep them safe even though they didn’t understand what was happening.
grounded_facts: Jane is present on Main Street during a crisis.
She actively participates in moving townsfolk to safety.
The townspeople are confused, indicating chaos or danger.
She works alongside Thor and Selvig in a coordinated effort.

memories.memory_text: I felt the chill take hold in the Vault, watching the guards shiver and huddle as the air turned sharp and still—then the shadows stretched long and deep, rising behind them like silent sentinels answering my unspoken call.
grounded_facts: A cold breeze began in the Vault while cheers echoed from above.
Guards reacted to the cold by rubbing their limbs and growing uneasy.
Large shadows appeared in the Vault, causing the guards to shout in terror and raise their weapons.

memories.memory_text: I saw the Destroyer standing in the shadows, its metal frame glowing with inner fire, holding the Casket of Ancient Winters like some sacred offering before setting it back on the pedestal.
grounded_facts: The Destroyer is present in the vault, holding the Casket of Ancient Winters.
The Destroyer moves the Casket back to its pedestal and returns to its post.
Loki observes this moment silently while others react with awe and shock.

memories.memory_text: Thor demanded war, shouting that he'd march on Jotunheim to break their spirits, but Father cut him down with a single look and the words 'You are not King. Not yet.'—and I stayed perfectly still, watching it all unfold.
grounded_facts: Thor argues for military retaliation against Jotunheim.
Odin rejects Thor's proposal and asserts his authority by reminding him he is not yet king.
Thor leaves in anger after being dismissed.
Loki remains present and observant during the confrontation between Odin and Thor.

memories.memory_text: I stepped away from the others, just long enough to speak quietly with a guard—no need for Thor to hear what I had to say.
grounded_facts: Loki physically separated from the group during their walk from the palace.
Loki had a brief, private conversation with a nearby guard.
Hogun noticed Loki's action, but no one else reacted publicly.
Loki rejoined the group immediately afterward without explanation.

memories.memory_text: I tried to steer Heimdall with words, to keep control of the moment, but he cut through my opening line with that cold remark about the cold—like he already knew everything. When he fixed his gaze on Fandral and mocked us with that absurd claim about hearing gas in Niffelheim, I felt the ground shift beneath me; my plan was unraveling in front of the others.
grounded_facts: Loki attempted to use diplomacy and subtlety to pass Heimdall's post.
Heimdall interrupted Loki by commenting on the cold of Jotunheim and implying knowledge of their true destination.
Heimdall made a pointed, mocking remark about sensing minor events across realms, unsettling Fandral.
Loki began to respond with 'You must be mistaken. We're not--' before being cut off by Thor.

memories.memory_text: I told the guard to go to Odin after we'd left. I said it plainly, and the weight of it settled in the silence that followed.
grounded_facts: Loki admits to informing the guard about the group's departure.
He justifies his action as a life-saving measure for the group and Thor.

memories.memory_text: I stared at my arm where the Frost Giant's touch had turned my skin blue—now faded, but the memory of its color lingers like a stain I can't wash off.
grounded_facts: Loki observes the blue discoloration on his arm caused by contact with a Frost Giant.
The skin has returned to normal, but he fixates on the change.

memories.memory_text: I touched the Casket and my skin turned blue—spreading like frost over my body—and in that moment, I knew something was wrong, something deep and irreversible.
grounded_facts: Loki touched the Casket of Ancient Winters and his body turned blue.
The transformation occurred as he lifted the Casket from its pedestal.
He questioned whether he was cursed, indicating shock at the physical change.

memories.memory_text: Odin admitted he found me in Jotunheim, a child left to die—Laufey's son—and I felt the weight of that truth: I was never meant to belong here.
grounded_facts: Odin revealed Loki was the son of Laufey, found abandoned in Jotunheim.
Loki asked why Odin took him, expressing confusion and emotional distress.
The revelation followed Odin's silence and visible weariness, which Loki interpreted as confirmation.

memories.memory_text: I asked her why he lied — why Odin kept the truth from me — and all she could do was say he wanted me to feel like I belonged. But the way she looked at me, the tears... it didn't fix the hollow feeling in my chest.
grounded_facts: Loki asked Frigga why Odin lied about his origins.
Frigga responded that Odin kept the truth hidden so Loki would never feel different.
Loki stared at Odin's motionless form after this revelation.
Frigga mentioned Odin could still see and hear them during the Odinsleep.

memories.memory_text: The guards knelt. They actually knelt. When that second one handed me Gungnir, I felt its weight — real, solid — and for the first time, I didn't have to reach for power. It was given to me.
grounded_facts: The Einherjar entered and blocked Loki's path.
The Einherjar then knelt before Loki, acknowledging him as ruler.
Another Einherjar brought Gungnir and offered it to Loki.
Loki reached out and took Gungnir, noting how it felt in his hand.

memories.memory_text: I sat on the throne, Gungnir in hand, watching Sif and the Warriors Three freeze when they saw me—my friends, so eager to speak with the Allfather, now forced to bow to me.
grounded_facts: Loki was seated on Odin's throne wearing ceremonial attire and holding Gungnir.
Sif and the Warriors Three entered urgently but stopped upon seeing Loki on the throne.
Loki addressed them with 'My friends... you haven't heard? I am now Ruler of Asgard.'

memories.memory_text: When Sif asked me to end Thor's banishment, I refused. I said my first command could not be to undo the Allfather's last, not with war looming and Asgard needing unity.
grounded_facts: Sif requested that Loki end Thor's banishment.
Loki refused, stating his first command could not be to undo the Allfather's last.
Loki cited the threat of war with Jotunheim and the need for continuity and unity in Asgard.

memories.memory_text: I walk beside Thor and Odin upon the Bifröst, seen by mortals in their myths, though they know nothing of the truth behind the image.
grounded_facts: Loki is depicted in an illustration in the book 'MYTHS AND LEGENDS FROM AROUND THE WORLD'.
The illustration shows Loki walking on the Bifröst alongside Odin and Thor.
The scene is observed by Selvig, not Loki, but the depiction includes Loki as part of Asgardian mythology visible to humans.

memories.memory_text: I told Thor Father was dead — watched his face crumple as he believed it, as he believed he was to blame. The weight of it crushed him, just as I meant it to.
grounded_facts: Loki informs Thor that Odin is dead.
Loki observes Thor's stunned reaction and emotional collapse.
Loki implies Thor's banishment contributed to Odin's death.

memories.memory_text: I called him 'brother,' said it was goodbye, and made him thank me for coming — then vanished before Coulson could see me. He never even knew I was truly there.
grounded_facts: Loki says 'This is goodbye, brother' and receives thanks from Thor.
Loki disappears before Coulson notices him.
Coulson re-enters and acts as if Loki was never present.

memories.memory_text: I let the blue creep across my skin in that ruined temple, watching Laufey's eyes widen—I'd never seen shock on a Frost Giant's face before. Then I smiled and called him 'Father,' just to feel the weight of it.
grounded_facts: Loki reveals his Frost Giant form by allowing the blueness to spread across his face in front of Laufey and the guards.
Loki says 'Hello, Father' after transforming, directly addressing Laufey as his father for the first time in the scene.
Laufey releases Loki from his grip upon seeing the transformation.

memories.memory_text: I told him Asgard was mine—watched him pause, consider, then accept. The way he bowed to my terms, not the other way around, made the lie taste sweet.
grounded_facts: Loki corrects Laufey by stating 'Asgard is mine' when Laufey claims Asgard will belong to Jotunheim.
Loki asserts control over the deal by dictating that the rest of the Nine Realms will be Laufey's only if he obeys.
Laufey ultimately accepts Loki's proposition after this exchange.

memories.memory_text: I saw the suspicion in Heimdall's eyes the moment I stepped out of the Bifrost—he knew something was wrong, that I'd been hidden like the Frost Giants.
grounded_facts: Loki emerged from the Bifrost and noticed Heimdall's glare.
Heimdall stated he could not see or hear Loki in Jotunheim and compared Loki's concealment to that of Frost Giants.
Loki perceived Heimdall's suspicion and responded with a challenge to his senses.

memories.memory_text: I reminded him of his oath—how Odin had no fear because Heimdall was bound to obey, and now that oath belongs to me.
grounded_facts: Loki questioned Heimdall about whether Odin ever feared him.
Heimdall responded that Odin was his king and he was sworn to obey.
Loki asserted that the same oath now applied to him, and Heimdall acquiesced with 'Yes.'

memories.memory_text: They whisper of treason and suicide, but I am not blind to their defiance—Hogun pulling the healing stones, the others conspiring in hushed tones. Let them think they move unseen; even now, my reach extends beyond the throne.
grounded_facts: Loki is on the throne of Asgard.
Hogun, Fandral, Volstagg, and Sif are in the healing room discussing finding Thor.
Hogun begins gathering healing stones, indicating intent to leave.
The group expresses fear of Heimdall hearing them, suggesting they believe Loki or his allies are watching.

memories.memory_text: They came to the Observatory and tried to reactivate the Bifrost against my orders. Heimdall refused to obey them, but they used his sword to bypass the controls anyway.
grounded_facts: Sif and the Warriors Three entered Heimdall's Observatory with the intent to reactivate the Bifrost.
Heimdall stated he could not open the Bifrost due to his oath to Loki.
The group bypassed the Bifrost controls using Heimdall's sword.
The Bifrost was activated without Loki's authorization.

memories.memory_text: The guard came breathless, reporting their absence—Volstagg, Fandral, Hogun, and Sif—all gone. In that moment, I knew they were no longer just disgruntled subjects, but rebels.
grounded_facts: An Einherjar Guard approached Loki and informed him that the Warriors Three and Lady Sif were missing.
Loki reacted with recognition, indicating he understood who was responsible for the escape.

memories.memory_text: I told Heimdall he was stripped of his title and no longer a citizen—my words cut through the air like frost, and for the first time, I let myself be seen not as a schemer, but as the one giving orders.
grounded_facts: Loki declares Heimdall relieved of his duties as Gatekeeper.
Loki states Heimdall is no longer a citizen of Asgard.
Loki speaks with authority, asserting his kingship directly to Heimdall.

memories.memory_text: I opened the Casket of Ancient Winters right in front of him, felt the cold surge up my arms, and watched the ice stop his blade mid-swing—so close I could feel the stillness where death had been a breath away.
grounded_facts: Loki uses the Casket of Ancient Winters to unleash freezing energy at Heimdall.
The cold from the Casket visibly travels up Loki's arms.
Heimdall is frozen in place, sword halted inches from Loki's throat.
Loki visibly sighs in relief after surviving the attack.

memories.memory_text: I placed Gungnir into the control panel and summoned the Destroyer with a gesture—its eyes flared to life, waiting for my command. I told it simply: ensure my brother does not return.
grounded_facts: Loki inserted Gungnir into the Observatory's control panel to activate the Bifrost.
Loki summoned the Destroyer using a gesture.
The Destroyer responded to Loki as its King.
Loki gave the order: 'Ensure my brother does not return.'

memories.memory_text: I pulled Gungnir from the control panel, feeling the hum of the Bifrost die beneath my hands, and looked up as Laufey stepped forward — then I spoke, calm and clear, 'Father. Welcome to Asgard.'
grounded_facts: Loki removed Gungnir from the Observatory's control panel, deactivating the Bifrost.
Laufey and two Frost Giants arrived via the Bifrost and stepped onto the platform.
Loki addressed Laufey as 'Father' in a deliberate, composed manner.

memories.memory_text: I fired Gungnir into Laufey’s back and said, 'your death came by the son of Odin.' The other Frost Giants attacked. I took one down with Gungnir. Mother killed the other and held me. I told her I would end the Jotun threat. Then I looked to Father and said, 'And I will make you proud.'
grounded_facts: Loki killed Laufey with Gungnir from behind.
Loki declared 'your death came by the son of Odin' after killing Laufey.
Two other Frost Giants attacked; Loki incapacitated one with Gungnir.
Frigga killed the second Frost Giant and embraced Loki.
Loki vowed to Frigga that he would end the Jotun threat.
Loki spoke to Odin, saying 'And I will make you proud.'

memories.memory_text: I rode hard across the Rainbow Bridge, the wind sharp against my face, and dismounted in one swift motion before stepping into the Observatory—no time for hesitation, not when the throne was within reach.
grounded_facts: Loki traveled on horseback across the Rainbow Bridge.
Loki dismounted quickly and entered Heimdall's Observatory.

memories.memory_text: I turned the Observatory's controls, feeling the mechanism shift under my hands as the great turret spun and locked into position—Asgard's eyes were now blind.
grounded_facts: Loki operated the controls in Heimdall's Observatory.
The massive turret spun and pointed as a result of Loki's actions.

memories.memory_text: I stood atop the frozen Bifrost controls and told Thor I never wanted the throne—only to be his equal—before demanding he fight me, knowing those words would cut deeper than any blade.
grounded_facts: Loki stands on the ice block covering the Bifrost controls.
Loki declares he never wanted the throne, only to be Thor's equal.
Loki commands Thor to fight him after striking him with Gungnir.

memories.memory_text: I used Gungnir to stop Thor from reaching the controls, then struck him and told him to fight me. When he refused, I immobilized him in the air and threw him down. He still wouldn't fight, so I taunted him about Jane Foster, and then we fought.
grounded_facts: Loki fires Gungnir to blast Thor away from the controls.
Loki strikes Thor with Gungnir and challenges him to fight.
Loki uses Gungnir to immobilize Thor in the air.
Loki flings Thor to the ground with a gesture.
Loki taunts Thor about Jane Foster to provoke him into fighting.
Loki and Thor eventually engage in combat.

memories.memory_text: I struck Thor down with Gungnir, sent him skidding across the Observatory floor, and watched his precious hammer fly from his grip—helpless as it vanished into the Bifrost’s storm.
grounded_facts: Loki used Gungnir to knock Thor back and disarm him.
Mjolnir was launched into the Bifrost vortex toward Jotunheim.
Thor failed to summon Mjolnir back initially due to the Bifrost's power.

memories.memory_text: I told him to die with the Jotuns—if he loved them so much—and saw the vortex pull at his body, stretching him toward oblivion, before that cursed hammer returned to his hand.
grounded_facts: Loki verbally expressed a desire for Thor's death.
Thor was physically pulled toward the Bifrost vortex.
Mjolnir returned through the vortex and reconnected with Thor.

memories.memory_text: I felt the weight of Gungnir in my hands as I drove it into Thor’s chest, lifting him off the ground—he looked at me, stunned, as I cast him across the bridge.
grounded_facts: Loki physically stabs Thor with Gungnir
Loki uses Gungnir to impale and throw Thor
Thor is bleeding and falls to his knees after the attack

memories.memory_text: I stood behind him as he swung Mjolnir at illusions, each failed strike feeding my voice—'I was always more clever than you'—until the circle of my images closed around him, helpless.
grounded_facts: Loki creates multiple illusions of himself
Thor swings at the illusions and fails to hit any
Loki and his illusions surround Thor with spears raised
Loki speaks the line 'I was always more clever than you'

memories.memory_text: I told the others we had to find a way past Heimdall — I knew we couldn't storm Jotunheim with his eyes on us, and I needed them to follow my lead.
grounded_facts: Thor speaks the line: 'We must first find a way to get past Heimdall.'
Thor is the first to voice a plan, initiating the group's covert action.
The group is assembling for a journey requiring secrecy from Heimdall.

memories.memory_text: Loki slipped away, spoke to a guard, and came back — I didn't know what he was doing, but I couldn't stop now, so I led the group forward.
grounded_facts: Loki leaves the group and speaks to a nearby guard while the others prepare.
Hogun notices Loki's absence and interaction.
Thor leads the group onward after Loki rejoins, maintaining momentum.

memories.memory_text: I stepped forward into the Bifrost, turned to my comrades, and called out, 'Come on. Don't be bashful,' before we were pulled into the vortex.
grounded_facts: Thor initiates movement into the Bifrost before the others.
Thor speaks directly to his companions, urging them forward with a grin.
The Bifrost activates and transports Thor and his group to Jotunheim.

memories.memory_text: I shouted at my father that the old ways are done, that he'd let Asgard fall while giving speeches, and in that moment I meant every word — I couldn't let fear rule us.
grounded_facts: Thor speaks defiantly to Odin, rejecting his passive approach to leadership.
Thor claims the old ways are over and that inaction will lead to Asgard's downfall.

memories.memory_text: I remember the weight of Mjolnir vanishing, the floor gone beneath me, and then falling — cast out by my father's voice, stripped of everything in an instant.
grounded_facts: Thor is cast out by Odin after their confrontation.
Thor experiences the loss of his power and is banished through the Bifrost.

memories.memory_text: I woke in pain, some mortal jabbing a needle in my arm—dared to touch the son of Odin without permission. I struck it away and demanded they bring me a healing stone, not this primitive torture.
grounded_facts: Thor wakes on a gurney in a hospital gown, in pain.
A nurse attempts to draw blood with a syringe.
Thor slaps the syringe away and angrily protests being touched.
Thor demands a healing stone instead of human medical treatment.

memories.memory_text: They kept coming—mortal after mortal—and I threw one hard enough to crack the wall, but still more piled on. I couldn't believe it—these weaklings actually forced me down… then the needle went in, and I felt my strength vanish before the darkness took me.
grounded_facts: Two orderlies attempt to restrain Thor after he resists.
Thor hurls one orderly into a wall.
Additional security guards and male nurses join to subdue him.
Thor is shocked at being overpowered by multiple humans.
The nurse injects Thor with a sedative.
Thor struggles briefly after injection, then loses consciousness.

memories.memory_text: I looked up at the sky and saw the blue expanse with a single sun—this is Earth, I realized, the thought grounding me like a thunderclap after the storm in my mind.
grounded_facts: Thor looks up at the sky.
Thor says 'Blue sky... one sun... This is Earth, isn't it?'
The scene takes place in a hospital parking lot on Earth.

memories.memory_text: I told Jane I would give her all the answers she sought if she helped me reclaim Mjolnir, because it belongs to me and is not just a satellite as they believed.
grounded_facts: Thor states his intent to reclaim Mjolnir, saying it belongs to him.
Thor corrects Jane's assumption that Mjolnir is a satellite, emphasizing it is not what they say it is.
Thor offers to provide all the answers Jane seeks in exchange for her assistance in reaching Mjolnir.
Thor speaks directly to Jane, showing sincerity in his offer and conviction in his mission.
tags: offering answers in exchange for help

memories.memory_text: I told her, 'You'll see soon enough,' when she asked who I really was—her eyes searching mine, uncertain but unafraid.
grounded_facts: Thor responds to Jane's question about his identity with 'You'll see soon enough.'
Jane is persistent in seeking answers about Thor's true nature.

memories.memory_text: I called it a rainbow bridge—watched her face change as the words left my mouth, wondering if she thought me mad.
grounded_facts: Thor describes the bridge as a 'rainbow bridge' in response to Jane's scientific interpretation.
Jane reacts with disbelief, saying, 'God, I hope you're not crazy.'

memories.memory_text: I saw the SHIELD agents distracted by the wreckage, so I ran for the open tunnel entrance and slipped inside before they could stop me.
grounded_facts: Thor observed SHIELD agents attending to the ATV wreckage.
Thor took advantage of the unguarded entrance to the tunnel.
Thor entered the tunnel on foot.

memories.memory_text: I punched one guard hard enough to drop him, then grabbed his body and hurled it into the others rushing at me—sending them flying and clearing my path.
grounded_facts: Thor punched a guard in the gut, causing him to fall.
Thor picked up the fallen guard and threw him at approaching agents.
The thrown guard collided with the agents, scattering them.

memories.memory_text: I told Selvig I had it all wrong — for the first time, I admitted I didn’t know what to do, and he didn’t mock me for it.
grounded_facts: Thor says 'I had it all backwards. I had it all wrong.'
Thor says 'For the first time in my life, I have no idea what I'm supposed to do.'
Selvig responds with encouragement about admitting uncertainty.

memories.memory_text: When that drunk man insulted Jane and challenged me, I chose not to fight — and then Selvig head-butted him and shattered his glass like a warrior.
grounded_facts: A drunk townie approaches Thor and makes a crude comment about Jane.
Thor says 'I will not fight him.'
Selvig steps in, head-butts the townie, and breaks his glass on the floor.

memories.memory_text: I reached into my pocket and gave Jane back her notebook, the only thing I could recover — I saw her face light up with surprise and gratitude, and I told her I was sorry it wasn’t more.
grounded_facts: Thor returns Jane's notebook to her.
Thor expresses regret that he could not retrieve more.
Jane reacts with surprise and gratitude upon receiving the notebook.

memories.memory_text: I took her pen and began drawing on the sketch in her notebook, showing how the Nine Realms are connected by the branches of Yggdrasil — I told her what my father taught me, and how her Hubble images are glimpses of those realms looking back.
grounded_facts: Thor draws the branches of Yggdrasil in Jane's notebook.
Thor explains the connection between science and mythology.
Thor references Jane's Hubble Telescope data as evidence of the Nine Realms.
Thor recounts his father's explanation of Yggdrasil.

memories.memory_text: I walked back to the diner with a new mug in hand, offering it to the woman I had wronged, and asked for her forgiveness—then invited myself back for more of her coffee, feeling something shift within me.
grounded_facts: Thor approaches Isabela with a replacement mug.
Thor apologizes for his prior behavior.
Thor requests to return for more coffee.
Isabela accepts the mug and responds politely.

memories.memory_text: I was drying a dish, caught up in this quiet mortal rhythm, when the door burst open and there they stood—Sif, Volstagg, Fandral, Hogun—my comrades, my family. I dropped the towel and ran to them, heart pounding with a joy I hadn’t felt since Asgard.
grounded_facts: Thor was engaged in domestic activity (drying dishes) when Sif and the Warriors Three arrived.
Thor immediately approached and greeted them with visible happiness upon recognizing them.
Thor expressed emotional relief and joy at seeing his comrades.

memories.memory_text: I told them I couldn’t return—that I bore the weight of my father’s death. Then Sif spoke, her voice steady: 'Your father still lives.' I froze. The room went silent. For the first time since my banishment, hope cracked through the guilt like lightning through storm clouds.
grounded_facts: Thor believed his father was dead and stated this as the reason he could not return to Asgard.
Sif directly informed Thor that his father was still alive.
Thor had a visible, stunned reaction to this revelation.

memories.memory_text: I saw the Bifrost forming in the distance—then it exploded down to the ground, sudden and violent, cutting through the moment like a warning.
grounded_facts: Thor observed the Bifrost storm forming in the distance.
The Bifrost funnel arrived abruptly with explosive force.

memories.memory_text: I told Sif and the others I must stay and fight — I am still a warrior, and I will stand with them.
grounded_facts: Thor declared he must stay and fight alongside the Asgardians.
Thor affirmed his identity as a warrior before changing his mind.

memories.memory_text: I looked at the townspeople and realized I had to protect them — I told Jane to help me clear the streets, and I vowed none of these people would die.
grounded_facts: Thor changed his decision after seeing the oblivious townsfolk.
Thor instructed Jane, Selvig, and Darcy to help evacuate civilians.
Thor declared he would not let any of the townspeople die.

memories.memory_text: I pulled Sif behind the burning car and told her to go while she still could — she argued, saying she’d die a warrior’s death, but I took her shield and told her to live to tell the stories herself.
grounded_facts: Thor finds Sif injured on the ground and moves her behind a burning vehicle.
Thor tells Sif to flee, insisting she survive.
Sif resists, declaring she will die fighting.
Thor removes her shield gently, persuading her to live.
Sif eventually nods in agreement and leaves.

memories.memory_text: After sending Fandral and Hogun away with Volstagg, I walked defenseless toward the Destroyer, offering my life in exchange for the innocents — I said I’d never return to Asgard, then spread my arms as it struck me down.
grounded_facts: Thor instructs Fandral and Hogun to take Volstagg to safety.
Thor claims to have a plan, giving them hope.
After allies retreat, Thor discards Sif’s shield and approaches the Destroyer unarmed.
Thor speaks directly to the Destroyer, apologizing and offering his life for the innocent humans.
Thor extends his arms as the Destroyer strikes him, causing severe injury.

memories.memory_text: I felt my breath leave me as my eyes closed, knowing I had no strength left to fight—but I stayed here, defenseless, so they would be safe.
grounded_facts: Thor's eyes close.
Thor's last breath leaves his lips.
Thor is defenseless and does not resist as the Destroyer stands over him.

memories.memory_text: I knelt and brought Mjolnir down hard on the ground—lightning answered me, striking the Destroyer as I rose again, fully the God of Thunder once more.
grounded_facts: Thor kneels and strikes the ground with Mjolnir
lightning strikes the Destroyer immediately after
Thor is now clad in full battle armor and holding Mjolnir
this moment marks his return to divine power

memories.memory_text: I drove Mjolnir straight into the Destroyer’s faceplate, forcing its own blast back into its core until it exploded from within—I felt the impact shudder through my arms as it fell lifeless beneath me.
grounded_facts: Thor flies downward with Mjolnir leading
Mjolnir collides with the Destroyer's energy blast and pushes it back
hammer is jammed into the Destroyer's faceplate
energy builds and explodes from within the Destroyer
Thor smashes it to the ground and removes Mjolnir afterward

memories.memory_text: I told Coulson we fight for the same cause — the protection of this world — and declared myself his ally, so long as he returned what was taken from Jane.
grounded_facts: Thor speaks directly to Coulson upon arrival via the Bifrost. Thor states a shared cause in protecting Earth. Thor offers alliance with SHIELD on the condition of returning Jane's items. Coulson responds that the items were 'borrowed'
not stolen. Thor links the return of Jane's equipment to continued research and SHIELD's sponsorship.

memories.memory_text: I took Jane in my arms and kissed her passionately, feeling the weight of what might come — knowing she fears I may not return, and that whatever fate awaits me, she is part of it.
grounded_facts: Thor takes Jane's hand and kisses it tenderly. Jane looks at Thor with fear of never seeing him again. Thor says
Whatever fate lies before me, you are part of it.
Thor then embraces Jane and kisses her passionately. The kiss occurs immediately before Thor and the Asgardians depart through the Bifrost.

memories.memory_text: I saw Heimdall fallen at his post and ordered the others to take him to the healing room — my brother will answer for this.
grounded_facts: Thor arrives at Heimdall's Observatory and finds Heimdall slumped over his controls.
Thor gives a direct order to have Heimdall taken to the healing room.
Thor declares Loki his responsibility and immediately leaves the observatory.

memories.memory_text: I told Loki I've changed — I wouldn't fight him, not even when he struck me, because I'm not the warrior who seeks vengeance anymore.
grounded_facts: Thor says 'I've changed' in response to Loki questioning his past desire to kill Frost Giants.
Thor refuses to strike back after being hit with Gungnir, choosing instead to fly past Loki toward the controls.
Thor explicitly states 'I will not fight you, brother' after being flung to the ground.

memories.memory_text: When Loki threatened to visit Jane, I charged at him.
grounded_facts: Loki threatens to 'pay her a little visit' after guessing Thor's change is due to a woman.
Immediately after the threat, Thor and Loki rush at each other.
The narrative states 'That did it' following Loki's threat, indicating it was the breaking point for Thor.

memories.memory_text: I reached out through the tearing force of the Bifrost, straining with every fiber to call Mjolnir back—not by strength, but by will. When I felt its weight slam into my hand, I knew I was still worthy.
grounded_facts: Thor attempted to summon Mjolnir after it was knocked into the Bifrost.
The Bifrost initially overpowered his attempt.
He later successfully summoned Mjolnir by concentrating and mustering his will.
Mjolnir returned through the maelstrom and flew into his hand.

memories.memory_text: The moment Mjolnir returned, it yanked me from the vortex’s grip—I didn’t think, I just flew, surging through the air straight at Loki.
grounded_facts: Mjolnir pulled Thor free from the Bifrost after returning to him.
Thor immediately took flight toward Loki after regaining the hammer.
Loki attempted to fire Gungnir at him during this moment.

memories.memory_text: I rose to my knees, blood on my chest, swinging Mjolnir at Loki after Loki—each one vanishing as my hammer passed through them, their grins mocking me until I called the lightning.
grounded_facts: Thor is injured and bleeding from a chest wound caused by Gungnir.
Thor attempts to strike multiple illusions of Loki with Mjolnir, but makes no contact.
Multiple illusory Lokis surround Thor, raising spears and claiming superiority.
Thor summons a bolt of lightning through Mjolnir that destroys all the illusions.

memories.memory_text: After the lightning struck them all down, I stood over the real Loki thrown backward on the bridge, dazed and defenseless, Gungnir knocked away—his lies shattered by my strike.
grounded_facts: The real Loki is sent flying across the bridge after the lightning destroys his illusions.
Gungnir is knocked from Loki's grasp.
Loki lies on his back, dazed and unable to move immediately after the attack.
Thor approaches and stands over Loki following the defeat of the illusions.

memories.memory_text: I stood on the balcony, telling my father I had much to learn, and that I hoped one day to make him proud—then he told me I already had.
grounded_facts: Thor was on the palace balcony at night.
Thor spoke to Odin about becoming a wise king and wanting to make him proud.
Odin responded that Thor had already made him proud.
Thor was consoled by Odin's words.
